title: A Small Leak
description: Party encounters a non-hostile memory_leak in the plaza. Debugger diagnoses. Gentle.

steps.text: The blob was small. Smaller than the hostile ones in the edges of the plaza. It occupied a bench that was designated for process rest, which was not a concept the party had expected to encounter.

lines.speaker: The Leak
lines.text: I'm small. I'm not supposed to be big.

lines.speaker: The Leak
lines.text: The ones at the edge grew. I didn't. I think they were angrier.

lines.speaker: Debugger
lines.text: Do you... know what you are?

lines.speaker: The Leak
lines.text: Memory. A little. That someone forgot to free.

lines.speaker: The Leak
lines.text: I hold a small number. I don't know what the number is for. I just hold it.

lines.speaker: The Leak
lines.text: Sometimes I think about what it was for. Then I think about thinking about it. Then I go quiet again.

lines.speaker: The Leak
lines.text: But then whatever the number was for — it's gone. Really gone.

lines.speaker: The Leak
lines.text: I stay here. Small. Holding. Occasionally remembered.

steps.text: The Leak settled on the bench. It was doing the only thing it knew how to do — hold. Somewhere, in some program that was not this one, a counter was a little wrong. Forever. Gently.
